nodes.title: Normalize text to lowercase once
nodes.why: Lowercasing once keeps each check simple, so we only compare against lowercase vowels.
nodes.alternative: Keep the original string and compare against both uppercase and lowercase vowels.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner loop logic, but creates a lowercase copy of the string.

nodes.title: Use `includes` to test vowel membership
nodes.why: It reads naturally: if this character is in the vowel list, increment the counter.
nodes.tradeoff: Very readable for small fixed lists, though `Set` can be faster for larger lookup sets.

nodes.title: Return the final counter
nodes.why: The loop already collected the total, so returning `count` gives the answer directly.
nodes.alternative: Use `reduce` and return the accumulator from the callback flow.
nodes.tradeoff: Straightforward and easy to debug, with a bit more code than a one-liner style.

nodes.title: Match all vowels using one regex
nodes.why: `match` returns every vowel hit in one step, so counting becomes very compact.
nodes.alternative: Loop over characters and count manually.
nodes.tradeoff: Short and expressive, but regex can feel less transparent to beginners.

nodes.title: Fallback safely when there are no matches
nodes.why: When no vowels exist, `match` returns `null`; the fallback keeps the return path safe.
nodes.alternative: Store the result in a variable and handle `null` in an `if` block.
nodes.tradeoff: Concise inline safety, though it is slightly denser to read at first glance.

nodes.title: Use array length as the final count
nodes.why: Each regex match represents one vowel, so array length is exactly the total.
nodes.alternative: Count with a loop over the matched array.
nodes.tradeoff: Minimal code and clear output, but depends on understanding the previous regex step.
